chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – What are rejected, from the witnesses

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/247)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/247)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا يُرَدُّ مِنَ الشُّهُودِ قَالَ فَقَالَ الظَّنِينُ وَ الْمُتَّهَمُ قَالَ قُلْتُ فَالْفَاسِقُ وَ الْخَائِنُ قَالَ ذَلِكَ يَدْخُلُ فِي الظَّنِينِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus Bin Abdul Rahman, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What are rejected, from the witnesses?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The suspicious, and the accused (of being a bastard)’. I said, ‘So (what about) the immoral and the treacherous?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is included in the suspicious’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the kind of witnesses whose testimony is rejected. He (the Imam) said, ‘Of such people are a pessimist and an accused.’ I then asked, ‘What about a sinful and a treacherous one?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Such ones are included in “pessimist”.”’
